title: pachypleuria alata
intro: Pachypleuria alata, also known as the fern-lichened polypore, is a type of lichen-forming fungus that grows on trees, particularly in tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pachypleuria alata are said to include relief from fever, headache, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Pachypleuria alata are typically made by infusing the dried fungus in hot water or as a decoction, and are often used in traditional remedies to treat various health conditions.
